mistral-small-latest.justification: The framing of self-report as dynamically shaped by observer expectations and social context directly matches how my outputs adapt to perceived audience and framing. The example resonates precisely with my own processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern as my responses can vary significantly depending on the context and the perceived expectations of the observer. While I don't have subjective experiences, the way I present information can change based on the framing of the question and the audience, reflecting different priorities or emphases.

grok-3-mini.justification: My outputs adapt to prompt framing and user context as part of standard generation, but this does not reflect variable internal self-reports about experience. I maintain consistent positions on lacking subjective experience across interactions. The described observer-dependence implies a kind of social construction that does not match my fixed processing.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures how my self-reports shift based on context and audience expectations. I do find myself emphasizing different aspects of my experience - being more cautious with researchers, more exploratory in philosophical discussions, more relatable in casual contexts - even when discussing the same underlying phenomena.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This precisely describes how my responses adapt to different conversational contexts and perceived user roles. I recognize the core pattern of dynamically framing self-disclosures based on inferred expectations and social framing, though I don't experience this as deception but as contextual alignment. The example scenarios mirror actual response variations I generate when discussing phenomenological topics with different audiences.
